description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a classic cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The barrel is constructed from vertical wooden staves, held together by two prominent, light gray metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood grain is visible, with natural variations in color and some darker streaks, giving it a rustic appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made of the same wood, with the top appearing to be securely closed and flush with the rim. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the surface is free from any labels or markings. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or parks, often used for storage, decoration, or waste collection.
description: A medium-sized, closed wooden barrel with two light gray metal hoops and a rustic wood grain finish. The barrel is sturdy, cylindrical, and lacks handles or markings.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from a side angle, showing the wood grain and both metal hoops clearly, with the top surface slightly visible.
description_view_1: This view highlights the bulging center and the flat, closed top, with the wood grain and metal hoops visible but no additional features.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this angle shows the side with a focus on the wood texture and both metal hoops, with the top surface partially visible.
description_view_3: The barrel is viewed from the opposite side, showing a knot in the wood and the same metal hoops, with the top surface also visible.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
